event: Neolithic farming communities settle the lands of present-day Bulgaria, among the earliest agricultural societies in Europe.
The Varna Chalcolithic culture flourishes, producing the oldest known worked gold treasure in the world.
Thracian tribes emerge as the dominant population across the region, forming a distinct Indo-European culture.
The Odrysian Kingdom unites many Thracian tribes into the first powerful Thracian state.
Philip II of Macedon subjugates the Thracians, bringing the region under Macedonian influence.
Rome annexes Thrace, fully incorporating the region into the Roman Empire.
Emperor Aurelian establishes the province of Dacia Aureliana, with the region becoming a key Roman frontier zone.
Upon the division of the Roman Empire, the lands of Bulgaria fall under the Eastern Roman (Byzantine) Empire.
Khan Asparuh founds the First Bulgarian Empire after defeating Byzantium, establishing a recognized Bulgarian state south of the Danube.
Boris I adopts Eastern Orthodox Christianity, converting the Bulgarian people and aligning the state with Byzantine culture.
Disciples of Saints Cyril and Methodius arrive in Bulgaria, where the Cyrillic alphabet and Old Church Slavonic literature develop.
Simeon I begins his reign, ushering in the Golden Age of Bulgarian cultural and political power.
Bulgaria wins recognition of an independent patriarchate and an imperial title for its ruler after the reign of Simeon I.
Emperor Basil II conquers Bulgaria, ending the First Bulgarian Empire and beginning Byzantine rule.
The brothers Asen and Peter lead a successful uprising, founding the Second Bulgarian Empire with its capital at Tarnovo.
Tsar Ivan Asen II defeats Epirus at the Battle of Klokotnitsa, making Bulgaria the dominant power in the Balkans.
The Ottoman Empire conquers Bulgaria after the fall of Vidin, beginning nearly five centuries of Ottoman rule.
Saint Paisius of Hilendar writes 'Istoriya Slavyanobolgarskaya,' sparking the Bulgarian National Revival.
The Bulgarian Exarchate is established, restoring an autonomous Bulgarian church and bolstering national identity.
The April Uprising against Ottoman rule is brutally suppressed, drawing European attention to the Bulgarian cause.
The Treaty of San Stefano and the subsequent Treaty of Berlin create an autonomous Principality of Bulgaria.
Bulgaria unifies with Eastern Rumelia, then defeats Serbia in the Serbo-Bulgarian War to secure the union.
Bulgaria declares full independence from the Ottoman Empire, with Ferdinand I proclaimed Tsar.
Bulgaria leads the Balkan League to victory over the Ottomans in the First Balkan War.
Bulgaria is defeated in the Second Balkan War, losing much of the territory gained the previous year.
Bulgaria enters World War I on the side of the Central Powers.
The Treaty of Neuilly imposes territorial losses and reparations on defeated Bulgaria after World War I.
A military coup establishes an authoritarian regime, suspending political parties and concentrating power.
Bulgaria joins the Axis powers in World War II, occupying parts of Greece and Yugoslavia.
Bulgaria refuses to deport its Jewish population to Nazi death camps, saving nearly 50,000 lives.
Soviet forces enter Bulgaria and a Communist-led Fatherland Front seizes power in a coup.
A referendum abolishes the monarchy and the People's Republic of Bulgaria is proclaimed.
Todor Zhivkov becomes Communist Party leader, beginning a decades-long rule as the country's dominant figure.
Bulgaria is admitted to the United Nations.
Bulgarian troops participate in the Warsaw Pact invasion of Czechoslovakia.
The 'Revival Process' forcibly assimilates the ethnic Turkish minority, prompting a mass exodus in 1989.
Todor Zhivkov is ousted, ending Communist one-party rule as the Eastern Bloc collapses.
Bulgaria holds its first free multiparty elections and renames itself the Republic of Bulgaria.
A new democratic constitution is adopted, establishing a parliamentary republic.
Hyperinflation and economic crisis lead to a currency board and sweeping market reforms.
Bulgaria joins NATO, anchoring its security within the Western alliance.
Bulgaria accedes to the European Union, completing its integration into Western institutions.
Mass anti-corruption protests sweep the country, demanding the resignation of the government.
Bulgaria receives EU approval to adopt the euro, set to join the eurozone in January 2026.
